display.purpose: Movie detail titles. Largest voice, once per screen.

headline.purpose: Home masthead and detail section headings.

body-italic.purpose: Taglines under detail titles. The only italic.

label.purpose: Tracked uppercase Brick Red eyebrows and category tags.

shadows.name: poster-rest
shadows.purpose: Resting lift under every poster. The system's only shadow at rest.

shadows.name: poster-press
shadows.purpose: Momentary rise on touch, paired with a 1.04 scale and a light haptic.

motion.name: enter-section
motion.purpose: Carousel header and content reveal on mount.

components.name: Search Bar
components.description: Paper field with hairline border; border and icon turn Brick Red on focus.
components.css: .ds-search { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; background: #F6F4ED; border: 1px solid #E4DED1; border-radius: 12px; padding: 8px 16px; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-search:focus-within { border-color: #B23A30; } .ds-search-icon { color: #7A7164; transition: color 0.2s; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-search:focus-within .ds-search-icon { color: #B23A30; } .ds-search-input { flex: 1; border: none; outline: none; background: transparent; color: #1C1815; font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 16px; } .ds-search-input::placeholder { color: #7A7164; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: Tracked uppercase Brick Red eyebrow used for carousel categories and detail sections.
components.css: .ds-label { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 3px; color: #B23A30; }

components.name: Carousel Section Header
components.description: Signature masthead device: Brick Red category label left, tabular counter right, hairline rule beneath.
components.html: En cartelera01 / 12
components.css: .ds-secthead { padding: 0 24px; } .ds-secthead-row { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; } .ds-label { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 3px; color: #B23A30; } .ds-counter { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 16px; color: #7A7164; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-rule { height: 1px; background: #E4DED1; margin-top: 12px; }

components.name: Metadata Chip
components.description: Read-only hairline-fill chip for runtime, year, status, language.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: #E4DED1; color: #1C1815; font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 16px; border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px; }

components.name: Search Result Row
components.description: Hairline-separated row: poster thumbnail, title, soft-brick year, two-line overview. Scales on press.
components.html: Blade Runner 20492017Thirty years after the events of the first film, a new blade runner unearths a long-buried secret.
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; gap: 16px; padding: 12px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid #E4DED1; transition: transform 0.15s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-row:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-row-thumb { width: 60px; height: 90px; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: #E4DED1; } .ds-row-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; } .ds-row-title { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 18px; color: #1C1815; } .ds-row-year { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 14px; color: #D98A82; margin-top: 2px; } .ds-row-overview { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.4; color: #7A7164; margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Poster
components.description: The only true card and the only shadow. Lifts and scales on press.
components.css: .ds-poster { width: 150px; height: 225px; border-radius: 13px; border: 1px solid #E4DED1; background: #E4DED1; box-shadow: 0 6px 12px rgba(28,24,21,0.15); transition: transform 0.12s, box-shadow 0.12s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-poster:hover { transform: scale(1.04); box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(28,24,21,0.30); }

components.name: Detail Back Button
components.description: Circular translucent-dark control floating over the hero image. The one dark control, allowed only on imagery.
components.html: <button class="ds-back"><svg width="22" height="22" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="19" y1="12" x2="5" y2="12"></line><polyline points="12 19 5 12 12 5"></polyline></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-back { width: 44px; height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); background: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s; } .ds-back:active { transform: scale(0.88); }

narrative.overview: An arthouse cinema's printed programme rendered as a mobile app. Warm paper stock, a single restrained ink, one heat of red reserved for headings and section tags. It is curatorial, not commercial: posters lead, type carries the hierarchy, and the chrome recedes until you need it. Lora, a contemporary calligraphic serif, runs the entire surface, with no sans and no second voice. The system rejects the streaming-app reflex (no dark UI, no glossy gradients), the TMDB/IMDB data-dump, and the dark-blue-plus-teal category default. The answer to 'movie app' here is paper and ink. Density is low and deliberate: three curated carousels, generous gutters, hairline rules instead of boxes.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper-and-ink palette, never pure black or white
One serif family (Lora) for everything: display, body, labels
Brick red used sparingly, on labels and section tags only
Hairlines and whitespace instead of cards and containers
Flat by default; only posters carry depth

rules.name: The Spot-Color Rule
rules.body: Brick Red is ink applied by a single pass of the press. It marks labels and rules, never fills a button, card, or bar. If red covers more than roughly 10% of a screen, it has stopped being a spot color.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Tone Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward the brick hue. Pure #000 and #fff are forbidden; the page is paper, and paper is warm.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Lora is the only typeface. No sans, no system stack, no monospace. Numeric counters use tabular-nums so figures stay aligned, but they stay in Lora.

rules.name: The Tracked-Label Rule
rules.body: Every label-tier element is uppercase with 3px letter-spacing and set in Brick Red. This is the system's signature; a lowercase or black label is wrong.

rules.name: The Flat-Chrome Rule
rules.body: Chrome is printed flat. Search bars, headers, chips, list rows, and nav all sit at zero elevation. The poster shadow is a deliberate, single exception, not a system of layers.

dos: Do keep Brick Red on labels, section tags, the masthead rule, and state icons only, never as a fill (The Spot-Color Rule).
Do set every label uppercase in Lora with 3px tracking and Brick Red color (The Tracked-Label Rule).
Do tint all neutrals warm; use Warm Paper (#F6F4ED) and Warm Ink (#1C1815), never #fff or #000.
Do separate rows and sections with 1px Hairline rules and whitespace instead of cards or boxes.
Do reserve depth for posters; let everything else sit flat (The Flat-Chrome Rule).
Do use skeleton rows for content loading and a Brick Red indicator for pagination.
Do keep the 24px gutter consistent across home, search, and detail.
donts: Don't make this look like a streaming app (Netflix, HBO, Prime, Disney+): no dark UI, no glossy gradients, no commercial chrome.
Don't drift toward the TMDB / IMDB data-dump: no dense tables, no cluttered metadata grids.
Don't fall into the category-default dark-blue-plus-teal movie app. The answer is paper and ink.
Don't introduce a second typeface. Lora carries display, body, and labels (The One Voice Rule).
Don't put a shadow on any chrome surface (search bar, header, chip, row). Only posters lift.
Don't use a border-left or border-right colored stripe as an accent on rows or cards. Use full Hairline borders or nothing.
Don't fill buttons, bars, or large areas with Brick Red; its rarity is the point.
Don't wrap text content in cards when a rule and spacing will do.
